content:
Hello! I am Edward Isayev, a 3rd year PhD student in the Mathematics Department of Boston College. My research interests are in algebraic geometry, especially enumerative geometry, log geometry, mathematical physics and the many interactions therein. I’m very fortunate to be advised by Qile Chen.
Before BC, I was an undergraduate master’s (MMath) student at the University of Edinburgh, from which I graduated with First-Class Honours in July 2024. I am originally from Ireland.
My CV is available in the top right-hand corner.
Feel free to contact me about anything either mathematical or otherwise at surname@bc.edu. I’m always happy to chat!
summary:

title: About Me

content:
This section serves as an archive for notes from various graduate-level topics courses I’ve taken over the years. In class, I use Goodnotes to write them up by hand, and I’ve recently embarked on a journey of Latexing them up. Progress will be slow, so if you need access to the raw handwritten pdfs, do reach out. Any errors made herein are entirely my own, and corrections would be greatly appreciated to my email.
Gromov-Witten Theory # Notes from a Spring 2026 course taught by Qile Chen. Topics include the fundamentals of \(\overline{\mathcal{M}}_{g,n}\), the string, dilaton and divisor equations, Frobenius manifolds, the Topological Recursion Relation, and Givental’s J-function. I will release them in chapters, and they should all be written up by the start of the next semester.

title: Course Notes

content:
Here you can find some expository notes on areas related to my thesis, which will eventually include toric geometry, orbifold cohomology, log geometry and more. They are informal, and have as a default example weighted projective space. Any mistakes made herein are entirely my own, and corrections would be greatly appreciated to my email.
Orbifold Cohomology (Forthcoming) # Notes from my attempts to learn Chen-Ruan cohomology. Filled with examples, especially various weighted projective spaces. These notes also explore the combinatorial description of Chen-Ruan Cohomology, in the sense of Coates-Iritani-Jiang.
